trigger.stage: post-coordinate-selection technical audit; before receptor preparation, redocking, expanded-train docking, or any new validation work
trigger.finding: The selected chain contains 328 C-alpha records and no other protein ATOM records. The original feature implementation consequently used each C-alpha coordinate as its missing side-chain centroid and created an artificial max-min distance.
trigger.consequence: The v1 structural selection is technically inadmissible for docking and is retained only as a failed audit.

scope: Add docking-input completeness checks only. Metadata filters, pocket residues, anchors, alignment, feature definition, pool size, max-min rule, and all data-use boundaries remain unchanged.

added_preparation_readiness_gate.rationale: The frozen receptor preparation keeps non-hetero protein atoms only. A candidate must therefore contain an all-atom protein chain, retain at least 95 percent of reference-pocket heavy atoms, and contain no backbone-complete modified protein residue that this preparation would silently delete.

decision: Run a separately versioned v2 coordinate selection. Do not substitute candidates manually and do not proceed to receptor preparation from the v1 selected manifest.
